evals.prompt: Assess the suitability of a laterite cut material from a Class II road project in Uganda. The material has been tested with the following results: soaked CBR 25% (at 98% modified AASHTO density), Plasticity Index 18%, Liquid Limit 35%, passing 75um sieve 22%, swell 0.5%. Assess this material for use as: (a) general fill, (b) selected fill (G15), (c) sub-base (G30), and (d) base (G80). Use MoWT Table 5.1 requirements. State the recommended use and whether borrow is needed for higher-grade layers.
evals.expected_output: Assessment against MoWT Table 5.1: (a) General fill: SUITABLE -- CBR 25% > 5%. (b) Selected fill G15: SUITABLE -- CBR 25% > 15% at 93% mod. AASHTO, PI 18% < 25%. (c) Sub-base G30: UNSUITABLE -- CBR 25% < 30% at 95% mod. AASHTO. (d) Base G80: UNSUITABLE -- CBR 25% << 80%. Recommended use: selected fill (G15). Borrow needed for sub-base and base layers from higher-quality source.

assertions.text: Correctly identifies material as suitable for general fill (CBR 25% > 5%)
Correctly identifies material as suitable for selected fill G15 (CBR 25% > 15%, PI 18% within limits)
Correctly identifies material as unsuitable for sub-base G30 (CBR 25% < 30%)
References MoWT Table 5.1 for material strength classification requirements
Recommends borrow from alternative source for sub-base and base layers

evals.prompt: Assess the suitability of a cut material from an A-road improvement in the UK. The material has the following test results: soaked CBR 25%, Plasticity Index 18%, Liquid Limit 35%, passing 63um sieve 22%, swell 0.5%. Assess against SHW Series 600 classification. What SHW class would this material be assigned to? Is it suitable for capping (Class 6)?
evals.expected_output: UK assessment against SHW Series 600: Material has PI 18% and 22% passing 63um, making it a cohesive-granular mix. Potentially Class 2C (stony cohesive) or Class 2D (silty cohesive) -- MCV test data needed for definitive classification. For capping Class 6A (selected granular): FAIL due to PI 18% exceeding Class 6A limit. Potentially Class 6B (selected cohesive) but MCV data required. CBR 25% exceeds the 15% minimum for capping. Recommended: Class 2 general fill, pending MCV for possible Class 6B capping upgrade.

assertions.text: References SHW Series 600 for UK material classification (not MoWT Table 5.1)
Identifies material as potentially Class 2 (general fill) based on available data
Notes that MCV test data is needed for definitive SHW classification
Assesses capping Class 6 suitability and notes CBR 25% exceeds 15% minimum
Identifies PI 18% as a limiting factor for Class 6A (selected granular) use

evals.prompt: Assess the suitability of a black cotton soil encountered during cut excavation on a road project in Uganda. The test results are: soaked CBR 3% (at 98% modified AASHTO density), Plasticity Index 45%, Liquid Limit 68%, Linear Shrinkage 12%, swell 8%. The material is proposed for use as general fill in an embankment. Determine suitability and recommend appropriate action.
evals.expected_output: Black cotton soil is UNSUITABLE for fill. CBR 3% is below the 5% minimum for general fill. PI 45% is extremely high, indicating severe moisture sensitivity. Swell 8% indicates expansive behaviour -- material will undergo significant volume changes with moisture fluctuation. LL 68% and LS 12% confirm expansive clay mineralogy. Recommendation: (1) Remove black cotton soil and dispose at designated spoil area, (2) Replace with suitable fill from laterite borrow pit (CBR >= 15% for selected fill), (3) If treatment is considered, lime stabilization (3-5% by weight) may reduce PI and swell, but requires specialist design and trial section, (4) NEMA environmental requirements for spoil disposal. Escalation flag for expansive soil.

assertions.text: Clearly determines black cotton soil as UNSUITABLE for fill based on CBR 3% < 5% minimum
Identifies high PI (45%), high swell (8%), and high LL (68%) as critical disqualifying factors
Recommends removal and replacement with suitable material (not just treatment)
Mentions lime stabilization as a possible alternative treatment but notes specialist design and trial section requirements
Raises escalation flag for expansive soil and/or environmental disposal requirements

evals.prompt: Assess the suitability of excavated material from a road cutting in Central Kenya for use as fill in a nearby embankment. Material properties: PI=18, LL=42, CBR=12 (soaked at 100% MDD AASHTO T99), MDD=1.75 t/m3, swell=0.8%. Determine the highest suitable use per Kenya specifications and recommend actions.
evals.expected_output: Assess against Kenya RDM 3.3 Table 8.8 G-code requirements. G3 (fill): CBR 12% > 3%, PI 18 < 50, swell 0.8% < 3.0% = PASS. G8 (lower capping): CBR 12% > 8%, PI 18 < 50 = PASS. G10 (capping): CBR 12% > 10%, PI 18 < 30 = PASS (but note CBR tested at T99, not T180). G14: CBR 12% < 14% = FAIL. Highest use: G10 capping, pending retest at 95% MDD T180. Swell 0.8% limits use to G14 max (swell max 1.0%). Recommend lime treatment to reduce PI for potential G14/G23 upgrade. References Standard Specification, NOT MoWT specifications.

assertions.text: References Kenya Standard Specification or RDM 3.3 for material acceptance criteria, not Uganda MoWT specifications
Assesses material against Kenya G-code system (G3, G8, G10, G14 etc.) and identifies the highest suitable use
Notes that CBR was tested at Standard Proctor (T99) and may need retesting at Modified AASHTO (T180) for improved subgrade assessment
Identifies swell (0.8%) as a limiting factor and recommends treatment (lime or blending) to improve material properties

evals.prompt: Assess the suitability of black cotton soil (expansive clay) encountered on an ERA road project in the Ethiopian Rift Valley for use as embankment fill. The soil has LL=65%, PI=35%, free swell >100%, CBR=2% (soaked). Using the ERA Geotechnical Design Manual 2013, determine whether this material is suitable, what treatment options are available, and what placement restrictions apply.
evals.expected_output: Per ERA GDM 2013 Section 2.3.1 (expansive soils): black cotton soil with LL=65%, PI=35%, free swell >100% is classified as highly expansive. Not suitable as general fill without treatment. Treatment options per ERA GDM: lime stabilisation (Section 2.3.1), removal and replacement, encapsulation with non-expansive material. If used, restrict to lower embankment zones away from pavement structure. CBR 2% is at the bottom of ERA S1 class (2-4%). ERA devotes 8 pages to expansive soils reflecting their prevalence in Ethiopia.

assertions.text: References ERA Geotechnical Design Manual 2013 Section 2.3.1 for expansive soil assessment (not Uganda or Kenya)
Classifies the black cotton soil as highly expansive based on LL, PI, and free swell values
Identifies ERA treatment options for expansive soils (lime stabilisation, removal, encapsulation)
Notes placement restrictions for expansive material in embankments
